ucs.title: [LLM] Turla / Secret Blizzard Outlook MAPI backdoor — outlook.exe loads or drops a non-Microsoft module from a user-writable path (LightNeuron / S
ucs.description: Hunts for outlook.exe loading or writing a DLL / scripting payload from AppData, ProgramData, Public or Temp. Turla (FSB Center 16; aliases Snake, Venomous Bear, Secret Blizzard, IRON HUNTER, Waterbug) is essentially the only state actor to weaponise an in-process Outlook MAPI add-in (LightNeuron, ESET 2019; the 'Snake' Outlook backdoor, NSA/CISA/NCSC 2023). The implant runs as a transport-agent DLL inside outlook.exe, parses incoming PDF attachments for commands, and exfiltrates results by emailing them back — all of which requires an unsigned/non-Microsoft module to be loaded from a non-syst
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.action) as action values(Filesystem.file_hash) as file_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="outlook.exe" Filesystem.action="created" (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Temp\\*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ps1" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.vbs" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.js" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.bat" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.scr") by host Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | where NOT match(file_path,"(?i)C:\\\\Program Files\\\\Common Files\\\\microsoft shared") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Path 1 — outlook.exe LOADS a DLL from a user-writable path
let OutlookLoads = DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "outlook.exe"
    | where FileName endswith ".dll"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Temp\")
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files\")
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\")
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, User=InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              Action="ImageLoaded", FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, MD5;
// Path 2 — outlook.exe WRITES a DLL/script to a user-writable path (catches LightNeuron deployment + bridges hosts without ImageLoad telemetry)
let OutlookDrops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "outlook.exe"
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
    | where FileName matches regex @"(?i)\.(dll|exe|ps1|vbs|js|bat|scr|hta|wsf|cpl|xll)$"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Temp\")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, User=InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              Action=ActionType, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, MD5;
union OutlookLoads, OutlookDrops
| where User !endswith "$"
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sysmon EID 7 (image load) — Turla Outlook MAPI backdoor catches DLL load by outlook.exe from non-system paths
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
| extend Image        = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded  = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         Signed       = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         Signature    = tostring(EventData.Signature),
         Hashes       = tostring(EventData.Hashes),
         User         = tostring(EventData.User)
| where Image endswith @"\outlook.exe"
| where ImageLoaded has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Temp\")
| where not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Program Files\")
| where not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\")
| where not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Windows\")
| where Signed != "true" or Signature !has "Microsoft"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, Image, ImageLoaded, Signed, Signature, Hashes
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Outlook Loads or Drops Module From User-Writable Path (Turla LightNeuron / Snake Outlook Backdoor)
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects outlook.exe loading or writing a DLL / scripting payload from a user-writable directory
  (AppData, ProgramData, Public, Temp). This is the on-host fingerprint of Turla / Secret Blizzard's
  in-process Outlook MAPI backdoors (LightNeuron, Snake Outlook backdoor) which run as a transport
  agent inside outlook.exe and parse PDF attachments for commands.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0010/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2019/05/07/turla-lightneuron-email-too-far/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-129a
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2024/01/01
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1547.001
  - attack.t1071.003
  - attack.g0010
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith: '\outlook.exe'
  selection_path:
    ImageLoaded|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\Temp\'
  filter_known_good:
    ImageLoaded|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
      - 'C:\Windows\'
  filter_microsoft_signed:
    Signed: 'true'
    Signature|contains: 'Microsoft'
  condition: selection_image and selection_path and not filter_known_good and not filter_microsoft_signed
falsepositives:
  - Per-user Outlook add-ins or third-party mail plug-ins (e.g. Grammarly, Boomerang) — baseline allow-list
level: high
ucs.rationale: Turla is the only public-attribution APT to operationalise an in-process Outlook MAPI agent (LightNeuron, ESET 2019; the Snake Outlook backdoor, joint-NSA/CISA/NCSC/FBI 2023 advisory AA23-129A on Operation MEDUSA). Generic 'unsigned DLL load' rules drown in dev-tool / Office-add-in noise; pinning the loader to outlook.exe AND a non-system path AND a non-Microsoft signature isolates the LightNeuron

ucs.title: [LLM] Turla 'Galaxy Opportunity' Andromeda/Gamarue USB hijack chain — rundll32 launches a DLL from removable media, then beacons to public C2 (Kaz
ucs.description: Hunts the very specific delivery chain that Mandiant attributed to Turla (UNC4210) in 2022-2023 and Microsoft re-confirmed under the Secret Blizzard cluster: Turla re-registered expired Andromeda/Gamarue C2 domains, profiled the still-infected USB hosts, and used the dormant USB worm to side-load Kazuar v3 / QUIETCANYON / TinyTurla-NG. The on-host fingerprint is rundll32.exe spawned by Explorer/cmd against a DLL on a non-system drive letter (D:-Z:), followed by that same rundll32 talking to a public IP within minutes.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_id) as process_id from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" (Processes.parent_process_name="explorer.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="cmd.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="userinit.exe") Processes.process="*.dll*" by host Processes.user Processes.process_id _time | rex field=cmdline "(?i)\s(?<drive_letter>[D-Z]):\\\\" | search drive_letter=* | join host process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(All_Traffic.dest_category) as dest_category from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.process_name="rundll32.exe" by host All_Traffic.process_id _time | rename All_Traffic.process_id as process_id | search NOT dest_category=internal] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// 1. rundll32.exe spawned by Explorer/cmd against a DLL on a NON-system drive (D:-Z:)
let RemovableRundll = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("explorer.exe","cmd.exe","userinit.exe")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b[D-Z]:\\[^\s\"]+\.dll\b"
    | extend DriveLetter = extract(@"(?i)\b([D-Z]):\\", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
    | project RunTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ProcessCommandLine, ProcessId, DriveLetter,
              ParentImage=InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
// 2. The SAME rundll32 PID then beacons to a public IP within 10 minutes (Kazuar / QUIETCANYON / TinyTurla-NG fetch)
RemovableRundll
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP))
    | project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, ProcessId=InitiatingProcessId,
              RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort, Protocol
  ) on DeviceId, ProcessId
| where NetTime between (RunTime .. RunTime + 10m)
| project RunTime, NetTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', NetTime, RunTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName, DriveLetter,
          ProcessCommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCmd,
          RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort
| order by RunTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sysmon EID 1 + EID 3 correlation: rundll32 from removable drive followed by public egress
let RemovableRundll = WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 1
    | extend Image       = tostring(EventData.Image),
             ParentImage = tostring(EventData.ParentImage),
             CommandLine = tostring(EventData.CommandLine),
             ProcessId   = tostring(EventData.ProcessId),
             User        = tostring(EventData.User)
    | where Image endswith @"\rundll32.exe"
    | where ParentImage endswith_cs @"\explorer.exe"
         or ParentImage endswith_cs @"\cmd.exe"
         or ParentImage endswith_cs @"\userinit.exe"
    | where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b[D-Z]:\\[^\s\"]+\.dll\b"
    | where User !endswith "$"
    | project RunTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, User, CommandLine, ParentImage, ProcessId;
RemovableRundll
| join kind=inner (
    WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 3
    | extend NetImage     = tostring(EventData.Image),
             ProcessId    = tostring(EventData.ProcessId),
             DestIp       = tostring(EventData.DestinationIp),
             DestHostname = tostring(EventData.DestinationHostname),
             DestPort     = tostring(EventData.DestinationPort)
    | where NetImage endswith @"\rundll32.exe"
    | where not(ipv4_is_private(DestIp))
    | project NetTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, ProcessId, DestIp, DestHostname, DestPort
  ) on Computer, ProcessId
| where NetTime between (RunTime .. RunTime + 10m)
| project RunTime, NetTime, DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', NetTime, RunTime),
          Computer, User, CommandLine, ParentImage, DestIp, DestHostname, DestPort
| order by RunTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Rundll32 Execution From Removable Drive Spawned by Explorer or Cmd (Turla Andromeda/Gamarue Hijack to Kazuar)
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects rundll32.exe being launched by explorer.exe / cmd.exe / userinit.exe against a DLL
  on a non-system drive letter (D:-Z:) — the on-host artefact of Turla (UNC4210 / Secret
  Blizzard) repurposing dormant Andromeda/Gamarue USB worm infections to deliver
  Kazuar v3 / QUIETCANYON / TinyTurla-NG.
references:
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/turla-galaxy-opportunity
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0010/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2024/01/01
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1091
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1218.011
  - attack.g0010
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\userinit.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)\s[D-Z]:\\[^\s\"]+\.dll'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  filter_known_installers:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'setup.dll'
      - 'autorun.dll'
  condition: selection_proc and selection_cmd and not filter_machine_acct and not filter_known_installers
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate vendor installers shipping on USB media
  - Field engineers running portable diagnostic tools
level: high
ucs.rationale: Mandiant's 2022 'Galaxy Opportunity' report (UNC4210) and Microsoft's Secret Blizzard updates document this as a uniquely-Turla pattern: instead of standing up new infrastructure (T1583), Turla compromises infrastructure (T1584.001) by re-registering expired Andromeda/Gamarue sinkhole-able C2 domains, then uses the still-active USB worm population to selectively deploy Kazuar/QUIETCANYON/TinyTurla

_field_issues.message: `Public` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`RemoteIP` is not a column of DeviceProcessEvents; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceLogonEvents, DeviceEvents, AlertEvidence, ASCDeviceEvents.
`RemoteUrl` is not a column of DeviceProcessEvents; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceEvents, AlertEvidence, ASCDeviceEvents.
`RemotePort` is not a column of DeviceProcessEvents; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceLogonEvents, DeviceEvents, ASCDeviceEvents.
`Protocol` is not a column of DeviceProcessEvents; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceLogonEvents, IdentityLogonEvents, IdentityQueryEvents, IdentityDirectoryEvents, SecurityEvent.
`RunTime` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`DriveLetter` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ParentImage` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ParentCmd` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `Microsoft` is not a canonical column of WindowsEvent.
`Windows` is not a canonical column of WindowsEvent.
`Sysmon` is not a canonical column of WindowsEvent.
`DestIp` is not a canonical column of WindowsEvent.
`DestHostname` is not a canonical column of WindowsEvent.
`DestPort` is not a canonical column of WindowsEvent.
`RunTime` is not a canonical column of WindowsEvent.
